confidence_rationale: Comprehensive Neuron review of the pleasure system as a network of interacting hedonic hotspots (NAc shell, VP, parabrachial, OFC) with a documented architecture in which liking signals modulate motivational and decision processes. The review explicitly addresses the question of whether wanting can occur without liking and treats liking as a critical input to motivational decision-making rather than a passive afterglow. For MECH-295 this is the cleanest theoretical articulation of the necessity claim available in the literature, anchored to the substrate evidence (hotspots) and the dissociation evidence (DAT-knockdown, dopamine-depleted rats). Confidence 0.76: high-impact venue, theoretically explicit on the architectural role of liking, but a review (not primary), and the strong necessity claim is asserted with caveats rather than directly tested.
failure_signatures: If the brain's reward architecture were a single value signal with no separable liking generator, the network of hedonic hotspots and coldspots described in this review would not be observable; it is.

If liking were merely a downstream readout of consummatory contact and contributed nothing to upstream decision-making, OFC pleasure coding (Kringelbach 2005) and OFC influence on choice would not show subjective-pleasantness modulation of value-based selection; the review documents that they do.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Pleasure (liking) is generated by a distributed network of cortical and subcortical hedonic hotspots (NAc shell, VP, parabrachial, OFC) that are anatomically discrete and pharmacologically gated. Liking signals are not passive readouts of reward consumption but active inputs to motivational decision-making, incentive learning, and conscious wanting. Wanting can occur without liking but the resulting motivation is impoverished, addiction-like, or directionless; full motivated approach to a learned cue requires the liking bridge to update the cue's incentive value.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-295 is the architectural commitment to exactly this picture: drive amplification (SD-012) cannot produce committed approach to a cue on its own; the liking-stream activation is the bridge that gives drive its cue-side pull, where the cue's incentive value has been previously shaped by liking experience. Berridge & Kringelbach 2015 articulate this as a general principle of brain reward architecture, with the substrate (hotspot network) and the dissociation (wanting can be elevated alone but is then impoverished/directionless) both anchored to primary data they cite. EXQ-483's failure mode -- drive amplified, override fired, approach not selected -- is the predicted signature of a system where the wanting circuit is engaged but the liking bridge is absent or unwired.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a high-impact theoretical review, not primary data. The strong necessity claim of MECH-295 (no liking bridge => no approach) is articulated in this review as a general architectural principle, but the review is interpretive synthesis of the underlying experimental literature, not a single decisive experiment. Daniel and the REE governance pipeline should weight the review for its consolidation power; the necessity claim's experimental backing comes from the cited primary papers (Pecina & Berridge 2005, Smith Berridge Aldridge 2011, Pecina 2003, plus the dopamine-depletion studies Berridge & Robinson 1998). The review also folds OFC pleasure coding into the network -- this is relevant to MECH-295's architectural placement of the liking-stream but raises an open question about whether the REE liking-stream should be specifically tied to hotspot subcortical signals or generalised to the OFC pleasure code.
mapping.source_context: Neuron review article; consolidates rodent hotspot mapping, primate VP firing, human OFC fMRI pleasure coding, and clinical addiction/anhedonia data into a unified pleasure-system architecture.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron review by canonical authors. Mapping fidelity high because the review explicitly articulates liking as a NECESSARY architectural component of full motivated approach, not just a parallel hedonic readout. Transfer risk moderate. Source quality high but consolidative rather than independent.
